{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided in Document [2], the white pulp of the spleen contains mainly white blood cells that are involved in the initiation of the adaptive immune response. Specifically, the marginal zone contains T-cells and the periarteriolar lymphoid sheath (PALS) also contains T-cells. However, the germinal center, which is part of the white pulp, contains B-cells. Therefore, the pulp proper contains B lymphocytes.", "answer_choice": "B"}
